M&A New York City, New York Charlotte, North Carolina Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania Job Description At K&L Gates, we are looking for smart, imaginative and hard-working people with diverse backgrounds, experiences and ideas to join us. Perhaps our search for talented visionaries and your search for important and impactful work lead to the same place. We are seeking a Tax associate to join the Charlotte, New York, or Pittsburgh ohice. The ideal candidate will have three to four years of experience in transactional tax matters, and experience with private equity transactions is preferred. LL.M. in Taxation is strongly preferred. 03/19/2026
Rochester, New York New York City · Intellectual Property · 2–4 yrs · 10 elite matches
Show job description
Patent Prosecution Litigation Rochester, New York New York City, New York Philadelphia, Pennsylvania Troutman Pepper Locke is seeking an Associate with 2-4 years of experience to join our Health Care + Life Sciences Intellectual Property Practice Group resident in our Rochester, Berwyn, Philadelphia, or New York ohice locations. Successful candidates will have experience with patent preparation, prosecution, and litigation. They will be forward thinking, thorough and detail oriented with substantive academic and professional knowledge. They will work well in a team environment and have excellent time management skills which they can utilize both independently and collaboratively in fast-paced situations. They must be licensed in the jurisdiction where they practice and have a background in biology, immunology, molecular biology or biotechnology. Candidates with a PhD in the life sciences are strongly encouraged to apply. Our dedicated team works with clients to protect lead

CHI Litigation Associate (4th - 7th Year)View Job ↗
03/19/2026
Chicago, Illinois · Litigation · 2–7 yrs · 14 elite matches
Show job description
White Collar Chicago, Illinois Paul Hastings is seeking an Associate with four to seven years of experience to join our litigation team in Chicago. This is an opportunity to be part of a rapidly growing practice and work on high-proﬁle matters for a collaborative team that prioritizes associate development and mentorship. Ideal candidates will have white collar experience. Outstanding academic credentials and law ﬁrm or governmental experience are required, and a federal clerkship and law journal experience are strongly preferred. Candidates must also be admitted to the IL Bar. Please submit a cover letter, resume, writing sample, and law school transcript. The salary wage range that we expect to pay for this position is a minimum of $310,000 and a maximum of $420,000 (annually). The actual pay wage may vary based on experience or other relevant factors. Show job description
Commercial Wilmington, Delaware Cozen O’Connor is seeking a litigation associate to join its Wilmington, Delaware ohice. The ideal candidate will have at least two years of litigation experience. The candidate should have demonstrated experience in all aspects of litigation, including drafting and reviewing pleadings, legal research and analysis, motion practice, and oral advocacy. This position may support attorneys in various practice areas across the ﬁrm. Must have superior writing skills, strong analytical and research abilities, and excellent communication skills. Admission to the Delaware Bar is required. The salary range for this position is $215,000-$255,000 and represents the Firm’s good faith and reasonable estimate of the range of possible compensation at the time of posting. Cleveland, Ohio · Litigation · 2–7 yrs · 24 elite matches
Show job description
Construction Cleveland, Ohio Ice Miller is seeking an associate with at least three (3) years of experience three (3) years of experience three (3) years of experience in construction law and/or litigation to join our practice. The successful candidate will become part of a collaborative team representing clients in a broad range of construction matters — including contract drafting and negotiation, proactive claims management during project construction, claims avoidance and mediation, arbitration or litigation, and related project development issues. Experience with mechanic’s lien claims is a plus. The ideal candidate will have prior experience in construction law, a solid understanding of construction project operations and administration, strong legal research skills, excellent drafting experience (pleadings, contracts, and other legal documents), and a demonstrated ability to communicate ehectively both orally and in writing. Commercial Miami, Florida Foley & Lardner is seeking an associate with 4-5 years of business litigation experience to join the Commercial Litigation Practice Group in our Miami ohice. 216 of 228 Construction Philadelphia, Pennsylvania Ice Miller is seeking an associate with at least three (3) years of experience in construction law and/or litigation to join our practice. Patent Prosecution Litigation Rochester, New York New York City, New York Philadelphia, Pennsylvania Troutman Pepper Locke is seeking an Associate with 2-4 years of experience to join our Health Care + Life Sciences Intellectual Property Practice Group resident in our Rochester, Berwyn, Philadelphia, or New York ohice locations. Successful candidates will have experience with patent preparation, prosecution, and litigation. They will be forward thinking, thorough and detail oriented with substantive academic and professional knowledge. They will work well in a team environment and have excellent time management skills which they can utilize both independently and collaboratively in fast-paced situations. They must be licensed in the jurisdiction where they practice and have a background in biology, immunology, molecular biology or biotechnology. Commercial Wilmington, Delaware Cozen O’Connor is seeking a litigation associate to join its Wilmington, Delaware ohice. The ideal candidate will have at least two years of litigation experience. The candidate should have demonstrated experience in all aspects of litigation, including drafting and reviewing pleadings, legal research and analysis, motion practice, and oral advocacy. This position may support attorneys in various practice areas across the ﬁrm. Must have superior writing skills, strong analytical and research abilities, and excellent communication skills. Admission to the Delaware Bar is required. The salary range for this position is $215,000-$255,000 and represents the Firm’s good faith and reasonable estimate of the range of possible compensation at the time of posting. New York City, New York · Corporate · 5–7 yrs · 5 elite matches
Show job description
Greenberg Traurig (GT), a global law firm, has an exciting full-time employment opportunity for a Mid-Level Associate in the Capital Markets Group in New York. We offer competitive compensation and an excellent benefits package. GT is consistently among the top firms on the Am Law Global 100; the Capital Markets Associate will have the opportunity to work with clients, large and small, from variety of industries across the country. Job Requirements: Ideal candidates will have 5 - 7 years of experience, from a peer BigLaw firm, in public and private equity and debt offerings, including initial public offerings and follow-on offerings, proxy statements and S-4 registration statements in connection with business combination transactions, interfacing with the SEC and other regulators, and familiarity with the ongoing reporting requirements under the ’34 Act for public companies, including 10-Ks, 10-Qs, 8-Ks and proxy statements. Experience in representing both issuers and underwriters would be beneficial. Candidates also should have day-to-day experience counseling public companies, including with respect to corporate governance matters. Candidates should possess a strong academic background, client management skills and drafting and negotiation skills. Candidates must have the skills to retain and grow existing clients and relationships. Candidates also should be admitted to the New York bar. The expected pay range for this position is: $365,000-$420,000. Salary will be determin

Greenberg Traurig (GT), a global law firm, has an exciting full-time employment opportunity for an Associate in the Environmental Practice of our Denver office. We offer competitive compensation and an excellent benefits package. GT is consistently among the top firms on the Am Law Global 100; the Associate will have the opportunity to work with clients, large and small, from variety of industries across the country. Job Requirements Greenberg Traurig’s Denver office is seeking an Environmental Associate with 4-6 years of environmental, natural resources law and related litigation experience. Qualified applicants must have excellent academic credentials as well as strong research, writing, and analytical skills. Applicants must also have education and practical experience in the areas of air quality, natural resource development, and/or federal or state administrative law. Experience with water quality, water law, energy project permitting, and/or solid and hazardous waste law is strongly preferred. Must be highly self-motivated, able to meet time-sensitive responsibilities and manage multiple work streams, hard-working, responsive and an excellent communicator, flexible, and willing to occasionally travel. A successful candidate will have extensive litigation experience; be capable of running and managing cases, communicating, and negotiating with opposing counsel, arguing motions in court; have strong analytical, research and writing skills; and be comfortable advising clients directly and managing deadlines and case dockets. Litigation Tampa, Florida Position Summary Our Tampa olice has an opportunity for a mid-level Associate to join the ﬁrm’s dynamic and long-established practice and represent premier local and national employers. This is an excellent opportunity for associates who are proactive and passionate about assuming a high-level role providing legal service within a sophisticated practice and who are seeking to assume a signiﬁcant role in all aspects of labor and employment law. This role requires extensive contact with clients, governmental agencies, educational institutions and opposing counsel. Duties include all aspects of corporate labor and employment legal representation, but are not limited to, developing creative ideas and pragmatic solutions for client problems, litigation and trial work, and resolving governmental and workplace investigations. Structured Finance New York City, New York District of Columbia Los Angeles, California Chicago, Illinois Charlotte, North Carolina Paul Hasting’ Structured Credit Group is seeking an Associate with two to six years of experience to join one of its CLO teams in NY. The ideal candidate will have experience dealing with credit products and collateralized loan obligation transactions and has represented investment banks (as both arranger and ﬁnancing provider) and investment advisors (as collateral manager and equity investors) in connection with various CLO and related warehouse ﬁnancing transactions. This CLO group is led by a Chambers Ranked “up and coming” partner with opportunity to work on a lean team and have client facing responsibilities and hands-on mentorship. This position may reside in New York, DC, LA, Chicago, or Charlotte, and candidates must be admitted to practice law and in good standing in the jurisdiction where they are hired. Chicago, Illinois Foley & Mansﬁeld, a professional limited liability partnership, is a national law ﬁrm with more than 110 attorneys in olices from coast to coast. We provide legal expertise, creative solutions and extensive trial experience for large corporations, small businesses and individuals. We have an opportunity for a defense litigation attorney to join our Chicago-based general liability team. The ideal candidate will have strong verbal and written communication skills, in addition to good academic credentials. The candidate must be a self-starter and possess the ability to multi-task. Working with others in a team-oriented environment is essential. This position is hybrid with ﬂexibility on the number of days in olice. Banking Corporate Corporate Investment Funds Finance Lending Leveraged Finance Fund Formation Acquisition Finance District of Columbia Cadwalader is seeking associates with 3-6 years of relevant experience for its Fund Finance practice. Qualiﬁed candidates will have experience in syndicated lending, commercial lending, leverage ﬁnance, fund formation, CLOs, asset-based lending, NAV ﬁnancings, or acquisition ﬁnancings. Candidates must possess excellent academic credentials and solid law ﬁrm legal experience. Selected candidates will get extensive interaction with preeminent bank, asset manager and lending clients. The anticipated annual base salary range for this position is $260,000 to $420,000. Atlanta, Georgia · Litigation · 2–4 yrs · 6 elite matches
Show job description
Atlanta, Georgia The Atlanta ocice is seeking an associate with 2 to 4 years of experience to join a national-caliber, high-stakes, litigation practice. Qualiﬁed candidates will have graduated from a nationally recognized law school in the top 10% of their class, been on the law review, moot court, or trial team, and served or are currently serving as a judicial law clerk, preferably for a United States District Judge. All candidates must have strong research, writing, and oral advocacy skills, have experience researching and drafting pleadings and papers concerning complex and dicicult legal and factual issues, be strategically and analytically minded, and be able to work both independently as well as part of a cohesive team, and be highly motivated with a strong work ethic. Candidates must be admitted to The Georgia Bar or eligible for admission in the near future. Interested candidates should submit their resume, cover letter, and J.D. transcript online through the GT website. Trans

P&S Finance Leasing Development Atlanta, Georgia Why Seyfarth At Seyfarth, we understand that great people are the key to our success, and we provide the opportunities to match. If you join us, you’ll work with state-of-the-art technology in a friendly and professional environment, and we will continue to invest in your professional development. If you want the freedom to grow at a ﬁrm that is invested in your future, keep reading. The Opportunity We have an exciting opportunity for a Real Estate Associate with 1 - 4 years of commercial real estate experience. The Day-To-Day As an associate in the Real Estate Department, you’ll have the opportunity to work on interesting, challenging, and groundbreaking cases in collaboration with senior attorneys. You will actively contribute to a dynamic department handling a broad range of matters for a diversiﬁed client base. New York City, New York · Real Estate · 6–8 yrs · 8 elite matches
Show job description
Leasing Litigation New York City, New York Applicant must have extensive experience and demonstrated success in drafting and negotiating commercial leases (with an emphasis on retail and ocice leases) from a peer law ﬁrm. Industrial leasing experience is a plus. Qualiﬁed candidates should have strong academic credentials, with 6-8 years of signiﬁcant New York City leasing experience, and a willingness to accept responsibility. Outstanding drafting, communication, client-service and negotiation skills are essential. Team members are collaborative and cross-trained in leasing, lease litigation (including commercial landlord-tenant litigation, construction litigation and other lease-related disputes) and bankruptcy. They are hyper-responsive to clients, and candidates must have excellent time-management skills (e.g., be able to multi-task and exceed deadlines). Must be admitted to NY bar. IP Litigation Associate MidlevelView Job ↗
2026-03-24
Boston, Massachusetts · Intellectual Property · 2–4 yrs · 2 elite matches
Show job description
Intellectual Property Litigation Litigation Patent Litigation Litigation Trade Secrets Boston, Massachusetts The Boston ocice of Greenberg Traurig seeks an IP litigation associate with 2-4 years of experience to join our trade secret litigation practice. The ideal candidate will have experience in litigations involving trade secrets, patents, and other IP at a mid-size to large law ﬁrm. The role will contribute to all aspects of litigation in large matters, including research; drafting; ESI collection, review, and production; witness interviews; depositions; and trial. The practice is fast-paced and involves frequent urgent or emergency proceedings. Candidates must be capable of managing a complex docket and be willing to learn new technology or subject matter quickly. Experience in specialized areas (e.g. biology, chemistry, AI, or computer code) is preferred. Candidates must have excellent academic credentials from a top-tier law school and strong legal writing and analyti

2026-03-24
Miami, Florida · Litigation · 2–7 yrs · 7 elite matches
Show job description
Real Estate Construction Miami, Florida Greenberg Traurig (GT), a global law ﬁrm, has an exciting full-time employment opportunity for a mid level Associate in the National Construction Practice of our Miami ocice. We ocer competitive compensation and an excellent beneﬁts package. GT is consistently among the top ﬁrms on the Am Law Global 100; the senior level Associate will have the opportunity to work with clients, large and small, from variety of industries across the country. The ideal candidate will have a minimum of three years of experience in construction or real estate litigation. Strong research, writing, oral advocacy, and discovery skills are essential. Ideal candidates will be self-starters inclined toward high volume, sophisticated casework. Must be admitted to the Florida Bar or eligible for admission to the Florida Bar. Litigation South Carolina Focused on employment and labor law since 1958, Jackson Lewis P.C.’s 1,000+ attorneys located in major cities nationwide consistently identify and respond to new ways workplace law intersects business. We help employers develop proactive strategies, strong policies and business-oriented solutions to cultivate high-functioning workforces that are engaged and stable, and share our clients’ goals to emphasize belonging and respect for the contributions of every employee. The Firm is ranked in the First Tier nationally in the category of Labor and Employment Litigation, as well as in both Employment Law and Labor Law on behalf of Management, in the U.S. News - Best Lawyers® “Best Law Firms”. Litigation Counseling Los Angeles, California Job Description: The Labor & Employment Group in the Century City and Los Angeles ocices is seeking an attorney with 3-4 years of labor & employment experience. The ideal candidate would have sucicient experience with a full array of labor & employment issues, including: defense of claims pending before state and federal courts; union management relations; reductions in force; employment contracts negotiations; policies and employee beneﬁts programs; and counseling human resource managers and supervisors regarding employee matters and compliance issues. Excellent working knowledge of California and federal employment statutes and regulations required. Admission to the California bar, immediate transferable skills, and a strong academic background with excellent writing skills are also required. The Opportunity We have an exciting opportunity for a Real Estate Associate with 1 - 4 years of commercial real estate experience. Leasing Litigation New York City, New York Applicant must have extensive experience and demonstrated success in drafting and negotiating commercial leases (with an emphasis on retail and ocice leases) from a peer law ﬁrm. Industrial leasing experience is a plus. Qualiﬁed candidates should have strong academic credentials, with 6-8 years of signiﬁcant New York City leasing experience, and a willingness to accept responsibility. Outstanding drafting, communication, client-service and negotiation skills are essential. Team members are collaborative and cross-trained in leasing, lease litigation (including commercial landlord-tenant litigation, construction litigation and other lease-related disputes) and bankruptcy. They are hyper-responsive to clients, and candidates must have excellent time-management skills (e.g., be able to multi-task and exceed deadlines). Must be admitted to NY bar. Litigation Counseling Los Angeles, California Job Description: The Labor & Employment Group in the Century City and Los Angeles ocices is seeking an attorney with 3-4 years of labor & employment experience. The ideal candidate would have sucicient experience with a full array of labor & employment issues, including: defense of claims pending before state and federal courts; union management relations; reductions in force; employment contracts negotiations; policies and employee beneﬁts programs; and counseling human resource managers and supervisors regarding employee matters and compliance issues. Excellent working knowledge of California and federal employment statutes and regulations required. Admission to the California bar, immediate transferable skills, and a strong academic background with excellent writing skills are also required.
